title: Aldama coraniana
intro: Aldama coraniana, also known as Mexican tea, is a shrub native to Mexico and parts of Central and South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Aldama coraniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to aid in digestion, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.

The genus Aldama is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, often associated with the family Malvaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure, which are similar to those found in other members of the Malvaceae family. Further classification may be necessary to determine the plant's exact relationships within the family.
